steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must compare fluorite (Mohs 4) and corundum (Mohs 9) specifically. The correct answer must name both minerals and use the scratch principle to show which is harder.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Corundum can leave visible scratches on fluorite, which means that corundum is harder than fluorite.: Names both target minerals, applies the scratch rule correctly (9 > 4), and states the conclusion directly. ✓
  • Choice B — A mineral with a Mohs number of 7, like quartz, is harder than one with a Mohs number of 4, like fluorite.: Compares quartz to fluorite, not corundum to fluorite. Wrong pair. ✗
  • Choice C — Corundum can leave visible scratches on quartz, which is why corundum has a higher number than quartz on the Mohs scale of mineral hardness.: Compares corundum to quartz, not to fluorite. Wrong pair. ✗
  • Choice D — The Mohs scale of mineral hardness can be used to order quartz, fluorite, and corundum by their ability to scratch other minerals.: Describes the scale generally without comparing fluorite and corundum to each other. ✗
A is correct.
